statement.p: One rectangular wall in your classroom is {13.75\ {\rm ft}} longer than it is tall. The total area of the wall is {246\ {\rm ft^{2}}}\text{.} How tall is the wall?

solution.p: The area of a rectangle is given by multipying its two dimensions. If we let h stand for the height of the wall, then {h+13.75} is the width, and the total area is {h\mathopen{}\left(h+13.75\right)}\text{.} So we must solve the equation {h\mathopen{}\left(h+13.75\right)}=246\text{.}
After multiplying and moving to standard form, we have {h^{2}+13.75h-246}=0\text{.} Using the quadratic formula:
Only the solution h\approx{10.25} makes sense in this situation. The wall is {10.25\ {\rm ft}} tall.
